title: Top Management Commitment
objective: Demonstrate visible, sustained leadership commitment to the management system; provide resources, set direction, and enable continual improvement.

title: Charters & Committees
objective: Operate formal committees with independent oversight for security, privacy, resilience, and AI governance.

title: Executive Leadership & Board Oversight
objective: Provide strategic direction and independent oversight of the management system, risk posture, and compliance through the Board or governing body.

title: Accountability
objective: Ensure every AI decision/outcome has a traceable human owner with authority to act on risk findings.

title: Fairness
objective: Adopt quantitative fairness metrics and enforce mitigation when disparities exceed thresholds.

title: Inclusivity
objective: Promote inclusive data practices with demographic representation and cultural relevance; engage diverse stakeholders.

title: Cultural Considerations
objective: Respect cultural norms and jurisdictional sensitivities in outputs, labeling, and UX; require contextual review before global deployments.

title: Tensions and Trade-offs
objective: Document trade-offs between accuracy, privacy, interpretability, and fairness; justify risk acceptance with leadership approval and transparent communication.

title: Transparency
objective: Publish explainability statements and disclose AI use where applicable; ensure documentation supports regulator and customer inquiries.

title: COC: Acceptable Use
objective: Ensure personnel adhere to rules for appropriate use of systems, data, and AI technologies to prevent misuse and ethical breaches.

title: COC: Data Ethics
objective: Establish a culture of responsible data/AI use that upholds fairness, transparency, privacy, and accountability throughout data collection and model development.

title: COC: Ethical Principles for AI & Tech
objective: Operationalize human-centric, ethical AI and technology use committing to fairness, accountability, transparency, explainability, and human oversight.

title: Core Policies
objective: Establish, maintain, and govern a cohesive suite of core policies across security, privacy, AI governance, ethics, and resilience.

title: Security Policies
objective: Define enforceable information-security expectations to protect confidentiality, integrity, and availability.

title: Response & Resilience Policies
objective: Ensure preparedness, continuity, and rapid recovery via IR, BCP, and DR policies.

title: AI Policies
objective: Codify principles for responsible, secure, ethical AI design, deployment, and operation.

title: Privacy Policies
objective: Define rules for lawful, fair, transparent processing of personal/sensitive data, aligned to AI privacy needs.

title: Ethics Policies
objective: Promote integrity, fairness, transparency, and human-centric technology use through enforceable ethical standards.

title: HR Policies
objective: Ensure HR practices support secure, ethical, and responsible conduct throughout the employee lifecycle.

title: Supply-Chain Policies
objective: Ensure suppliers and partners meet organizational security, privacy, and ethical standards throughout the contract lifecycle.

title: Data-Governance Policies
objective: Ensure data quality, integrity, lifecycle management, and responsible use across operations and AI systems.

title: Core Standards
objective: Define and maintain organizational standards that operationalize policy commitments into measurable, auditable controls.

title: Security Standards
objective: Establish enforceable technical/procedural standards protecting systems and data, including AI-specific security requirements.

title: Response & Resilience Standards
objective: Define standardized processes/criteria for incident response, DR, and business continuity including AI-specific failure modes.

title: AI Standards
objective: Translate AI policy into actionable, measurable standards governing data, model development/testing, deployment, monitoring, transparency, and retirement.

title: Data Privacy Compliance
objective: Comply with applicable data-protection/privacy laws with demonstrable accountability and oversight.

title: Intellectual Property (IP) Management
objective: Protect IP assets—including proprietary data, algorithms, code, documentation, and AI models—while respecting third-party rights.

title: AI Regulatory Compliance
objective: Ensure AI systems conform to applicable legal obligations for transparency, accountability, oversight, and safety.

title: Cybersecurity Regulatory Compliance
objective: Align cybersecurity controls with legal, contractual, and regulatory obligations and maintain continuous compliance.

title: Ethical Use, Fairness & Bias Compliance
objective: Ensure technology and AI practices are fair, transparent, and non-discriminatory.

title: Org Values & Governance
objective: Establish and embed organizational values and ethical tenets guiding secure, ethical, and equitable design/deployment of AI and digital systems.

title: Privacy Program
objective: Establish and maintain an enterprise-wide Privacy Management Program ensuring lawful, ethical, transparent handling of personal/sensitive data.

title: Risk Management Framework
objective: Establish a unified ERM framework integrating security, AI, privacy, and operational risks.

title: Risk Appetite and Tolerance
objective: Define and approve thresholds for acceptable risk across domains.

title: Risk Management Program: AI
objective: Operationalize an AI-specific risk-management program across lifecycle.

title: Risk-Management Program: Cybersecurity
objective: Continuously identify, analyze, and mitigate cybersecurity risks aligned to threat intel, business impact, and obligations.

title: Risk-Management Program: Third-Party
objective: Identify and manage risks posed by vendors/partners to protect data, systems, and AI integrity.

title: System Boundaries
objective: Establish precise technical/physical boundaries and interfaces so controls can be correctly applied, tested, and audited.

title: Mission Statement
objective: Define and communicate the organization’s purpose and mission so that all security, privacy, resilience, and AI governance activities are clearly aligned to the value the organization delivers and the risks it accepts.

title: Goals & Context
objective: Translate mission into concrete governance goals by evaluating internal/external context, stakeholder expectations, and constraints; ensure objectives are measurable and risk-informed.

title: AI Scope
objective: Identify all AI systems and activities under organizational control, categorize them by risk, and declare lifecycle coverage for governance and assurance.

title: Security Scope
objective: Establish which assets and environments are protected by the security program and how protection is proportionate to risk and criticality.

title: Regulatory Scope
objective: Determine applicable legal, contractual, and customer requirements and ensure the management system addresses them.
